Alzheimer.name: Alzheimer's Disease
Alzheimer.description: A progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by memory loss, cognitive decline, and behavioral changes. It is the most common cause of dementia.

Malaria.name: Malaria
Malaria.description: An infectious disease caused by protozoan parasites transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes. It causes fever, chills, and flu-like symptoms.

Allergy.name: Allergy
Allergy.description: An immune system response to a foreign substance that is not typically harmful to the body. Can include reactions to foods, pollen, or pet dander.

Hypothyroidism.name: Hypothyroidism
Hypothyroidism.description: A disorder where the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormone, leading to slowed metabolism and various symptoms.

Psoriasis.name: Psoriasis
Psoriasis.description: A chronic skin disorder that forms thick, red, bumpy patches covered with silvery scales. Most commonly appears on scalp, elbows, knees, and lower back.
precautions: wash hands with warm soapy water

GERD.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
GERD.description: A digestive disorder affecting the lower esophageal sphincter, causing acid reflux and heartburn.

Chronic cholestasis.name: Chronic Cholestasis
Chronic cholestasis.description: A chronic liver condition characterized by defective bile acid transport from the liver to the intestine.

Hepatitis.name: Hepatitis
Hepatitis.description: Inflammation of the liver caused by viral infection. Multiple types exist (A, B, C, D, E).

Osteoarthritis.name: Osteoarthritis
Osteoarthritis.description: The most common form of arthritis, occurring when protective cartilage that cushions bone ends wears down over time.

Vertigo.name: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV)
Vertigo.description: One of the most common causes of vertigo, causing brief episodes of mild to intense dizziness.

Hypoglycemia.name: Hypoglycemia
Hypoglycemia.description: A condition where blood sugar level is lower than normal, often related to diabetes treatment.

Acne.name: Acne
Acne.description: Formation of comedones, papules, pustules, nodules, and cysts as a result of obstruction and inflammation of hair follicles.

Diabetes.name: Diabetes
Diabetes.description: A disease where blood glucose is too high. Insulin helps glucose from food get into cells to be used for energy.

Impetigo.name: Impetigo
Impetigo.description: A common and highly contagious skin infection mainly affecting infants and children, appearing as red sores.
precautions: soak affected area in warm water

Hypertension.name: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
Hypertension.description: A long-term medical condition where blood pressure in arteries is persistently elevated.

Peptic ulcer disease.name: Peptic Ulcer Disease
Peptic ulcer disease.description: A break in the inner lining of the stomach, first part of small intestine, or lower esophagus.

Hemorrhoids.name: Dimorphic Hemorrhoids (Piles)
Hemorrhoids.description: Vascular structures in the anal canal that become enlarged and inflamed.

Common cold.name: Common Cold
Common cold.description: A viral infection of the nose and throat (upper respiratory tract), usually harmless.

Chicken pox.name: Chicken Pox
Chicken pox.description: A highly contagious disease caused by varicella-zoster virus, causing itchy blister-like rash.

Cervical spondylosis.name: Cervical Spondylosis
Cervical spondylosis.description: Age-related wear and tear affecting spinal disks in the neck, causing osteoarthritis.
precautions: use heating pad or cold pack

Hyperthyroidism.name: Hyperthyroidism
Hyperthyroidism.description: A condition where thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, accelerating metabolism.

Urinary tract infection.name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
Urinary tract infection.description: An infection of the kidney, ureter, bladder, or urethra.

Varicose veins.name: Varicose Veins
Varicose veins.description: Enlarged and twisted veins, appearing as bulging blue blood vessels visible through skin.
precautions: lie down flat and raise leg high

AIDS.name: AIDS (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome)
AIDS.description: A chronic, potentially life-threatening condition caused by HIV, damaging the immune system.

Paralysis.name: Paralysis (Brain Hemorrhage)
Paralysis.description: Intracerebral hemorrhage when blood bursts into brain tissue, causing damage.

Typhoid.name: Typhoid
Typhoid.description: An acute illness characterized by fever caused by Salmonella typhi bacteria.

Hepatitis B.name: Hepatitis B
Hepatitis B.description: An infection of the liver caused by hepatitis B virus, spread through blood and body fluids.

Hepatitis A.name: Hepatitis A
Hepatitis A.description: A highly contagious liver infection caused by hepatitis A virus.

Fungal infection.name: Fungal Infection
Fungal infection.description: An infection when an invading fungus takes over an area of the body.
precautions: use detol or neem in bathing water

Hepatitis C.name: Hepatitis C
Hepatitis C.description: Inflammation of the liver due to hepatitis C virus, usually spread via blood.

Migraine.name: Migraine
Migraine.description: A neurological condition causing severe throbbing pain, usually on one side of the head.

Bronchial asthma.name: Bronchial Asthma
Bronchial asthma.description: A medical condition causing airway swelling and narrowing, making breathing difficult.

Alcoholic hepatitis.name: Alcoholic Hepatitis
Alcoholic hepatitis.description: A diseased, inflammatory condition of the liver caused by heavy alcohol consumption.

Jaundice.name: Jaundice
Jaundice.description: Yellow staining of skin and eyes by abnormally high blood levels of bile pigment bilirubin.
precautions: eat fruits and high fiber food

Hepatitis E.name: Hepatitis E
Hepatitis E.description: A rare form of liver inflammation caused by hepatitis E virus, transmitted via food or water.

Dengue.name: Dengue
Dengue.description: An acute infectious disease caused by dengue virus, transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes.

Hepatitis D.name: Hepatitis D
Hepatitis D.description: An infection causing liver inflammation, also known as hepatitis delta virus.

Heart attack.name: Heart Attack
Heart attack.description: Death of heart muscle due to loss of blood supply, usually caused by coronary artery blockage.

Pneumonia.name: Pneumonia
Pneumonia.description: An infection in one or both lungs caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi.

Arthritis.name: Arthritis
Arthritis.description: Swelling and tenderness of one or more joints, with main symptoms being pain and stiffness.

Gastroenteritis.name: Gastroenteritis
Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the digestive tract, particularly stomach and intestines.
precautions: stop eating solid food for a while

Tuberculosis.name: Tuberculosis (TB)
Tuberculosis.description: An infectious disease usually caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria.

Drug reaction.name: Drug Reaction
Drug reaction.description: An injury caused by taking medication, may occur from single dose or prolonged use.
